tell: over-descriptive / verbose names (handleSubmitButtonClick, getUserDataFromApi)
emoji in code / console logs / commit messages (✅ 🚀 🎉)
boilerplate / scaffolding / tutorial-shaped code
markdown / code-fence backticks or '```python' left in the file
explanatory 'Note:' / 'Remember:' / 'Keep in mind' preambles in the code
over-engineered / needless abstraction for a simple task
placeholder / ellipsis comments ('// rest of your code', '// ... your logic here')
model self-disclosure / refusal text pasted into code ('As an AI', 'I cannot')
print/console.log debugging left everywhere; 'Successfully ...' messages
narrating / step-by-step comments ('# Step 1', '# Now we...')
too clean / too perfect / suspiciously consistent formatting
catch-all / swallowed errors ('except Exception', bare catch)
over-defensive: null/None checks & validation for impossible cases
chat preamble left in: 'Here's the complete/updated code', 'Sure! Here is'
everything split into tiny helper functions
Python: if __name__ == '__main__' / dataclasses / main() on trivial scripts
type hints / annotations on everything (incl. dynamically-typed langs)
over-commenting / a comment on every line
leftover TODO/placeholder ('# TODO: implement', '// your code here')
comments that restate / explain the obvious
try/except (try/catch) wrapped around everything
generic placeholder names (data, result, temp, item, foo, value)
docstring/JSDoc on every trivial function
style doesn't match the rest of the codebase
reimplements something the stdlib/library already provides
over-use of f-strings / list comprehensions / one-liners
JS/TS: async/await, const, arrow funcs, optional chaining everywhere
unnecessary semicolons / explicit returns / verbose typing idioms
